evals.prompt: Should we pivot from B2B SaaS to a B2C model for our project management tool? We have 200 enterprise customers paying $50K/yr ARR average, but consumer demand signals are strong — 10K waitlist signups from a Product Hunt launch. Team is 30 people, $2M ARR, 18 months runway.
evals.expected_output: A 4-round adversarial debate with Visionary/Skeptic/Pragmatist personas producing a decision document with verdict, action items, confidence score, and debate metrics.

assertions.text: Output contains all 4 rounds (Round 1 through Round 4)
Visionary, Skeptic, and Pragmatist personas all appear and are distinct
Round 1 includes a thesis, rationale with 3-5 arguments, timeline, resources needed, and upside scenario
Round 2 contains numbered challenges with severity ratings (critical/significant/minor)
Round 3 addresses each numbered challenge from Round 2, with concessions or counters
Round 4 contains a verdict that is one of: proceed, modify-and-proceed, defer, reject
Round 4 includes numbered action items with who/what/when
Debate metrics table with challengesRaised, challengesSurvived, challengesDefeated, challengesConceded, confidenceScore
Round 4 includes a risks accepted section referencing specific challenges
Round 4 includes a review date for revisiting the decision

evals.prompt: Should we acquire a 5-person AI startup for $3M to accelerate our ML capabilities, or hire 3 senior ML engineers at $250K each? We're a 50-person company with $8M ARR, no ML expertise in-house, and our biggest customer is asking for predictive features by Q4.
evals.expected_output: A 4-round adversarial debate about build-vs-buy for ML capabilities producing a decision document.

assertions.text: Output contains all 4 rounds (Round 1 through Round 4)
Visionary, Skeptic, and Pragmatist personas all appear and are distinct
Round 2 challenges are specific to the proposal (not generic pessimism), referencing details like cost, integration risk, or team dynamics
Round 3 references specific challenge numbers from Round 2
Round 4 contains one of the 4 valid verdicts (proceed/modify-and-proceed/defer/reject)
Debate metrics table with all 5 metrics filled in
Numbered action items with concrete next steps
Round 4 rationale references arguments from both Visionary and Skeptic

evals.prompt: We're considering raising a Series A at $15M pre-money. We're profitable at $4M ARR with 40% margins, growing 10% MoM. The VC wants 20% equity and a board seat. Should we take the money or stay bootstrapped?
evals.expected_output: A 4-round adversarial debate about fundraising vs bootstrapping producing a decision document with clear verdict.

assertions.text: Output contains all 4 rounds (Round 1 through Round 4)
Visionary, Skeptic, and Pragmatist personas all appear and are distinct
Every challenge in Round 2 has a severity rating (critical/significant/minor)
Round 3 contains at least one concession where the Visionary acknowledges a valid challenge
Round 4 contains one of the 4 valid verdicts
Debate metrics include a confidenceScore between 1-10
A review date is specified for revisiting the decision
Risks accepted section exists with specific risk references
